decisions.title: Hardcoded baseline exclude patterns
decisions.decision: Use a hardcoded SHIP_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS list in the module. Ship safety should not depend on repo-specific ignore config. Runtime paths: .cnogo/runs/, .cnogo/work-orders/ (except .gitkeep), .cnogo/feature-phases.json, .cnogo/watch/, .cnogo/worktree-session.json. Additive config may come later but the baseline is not replaceable.
decisions.rationale: Some runtime files are operational even if they exist in the repo. Safety baseline must be hardcoded.

decisions.title: Commit message from plan contract
decisions.decision: Read the plan's commitMessage field as source of truth. If multiple plans, use the latest. If missing, derive from conventional commit prefix + feature slug + plan goal. No LLM re-summarization at ship time.
decisions.rationale: Commit messages are authored during /plan when context is fresh. Re-generating at ship time risks drift.

decisions.title: Terse deterministic PR body
decisions.decision: PR body format: ## Summary (bullets from plan goals), ## Test Plan (from SUMMARY.json verification), ## Review (verdict + reviewer names from REVIEW.json), ## Planning References (repo-relative artifact paths). Add ## Follow-ups section only when review verdict is warn or there are open items. No tables, no LLM prose, no GitHub artifact links in v1.
decisions.rationale: Deterministic output is reviewable and reproducible. GitHub links add coupling too early.

decisions.title: Auto-infer on run-ship-complete by default
decisions.decision: run-ship-complete auto-infers branch and commit SHA by default. Fails closed unless: current branch is exactly feature/<feature>, git rev-parse HEAD succeeds, and there is no ambiguity about the target run. If any guard fails, require explicit args.
decisions.rationale: Reduces manual copy-paste friction while maintaining safety through strict guards.

decisions.title: New module in orchestration package
decisions.decision: Code lives in .cnogo/scripts/workflow/orchestration/ship_draft.py. Public API: build_ship_draft(root, feature) -> dict, compute_commit_surface(root, feature) -> list, generate_pr_body(root, feature) -> str, generate_commit_message(root, feature) -> str. CLI: run-ship-draft subcommand in workflow_memory.py.
decisions.rationale: Ship draft is above the ship state machine — it reads plans, summaries, and reviews to compose outputs.

decisions.title: Update /ship command to use ship draft
decisions.decision: Replace manual commit/push/PR steps in ship.md with run-ship-draft --json output driving git add, git commit, git push, gh pr create, and run-ship-complete. Still show each step explicitly but with pre-computed values.
decisions.rationale: Guided ship path reduces errors while keeping each step visible and auditable.

decisions.title: Work Orders are runtime, not shipped source
decisions.decision: Keep .cnogo/work-orders/*.json gitignored and excluded from ship. Only .gitkeep is tracked. If auditable published state is needed later, create a separate explicit artifact.
decisions.rationale: Work Orders are operational state derived from delivery runs. Committing them creates noise and merge conflicts.

decisions.title: Commit surface output includes convenience command
decisions.decision: run-ship-draft returns both commitSurface[] (canonical file list, source of truth) and gitAddCommand (convenience string for operator). The file list is authoritative; the command is just help.
decisions.rationale: Operators need both: the list for review, the command for execution.

constraints: Python stdlib only — no external dependencies
No git operations in the module — compute surfaces from file state and plan contracts, let /ship do git calls
Plan commitMessage is source of truth — no LLM re-summarization
Backward compatible — /ship still works manually if run-ship-draft is not used
SHIP_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS baseline is hardcoded and not replaceable

relatedCode.description: Ship state machine — start_ship, complete_ship, fail_ship, sync_ship_state
DeliveryRun model — ship dict, plan_path, tasks with filePaths
Ship readiness gate — 20 checks for review, profile, and delivery run state
CLI entrypoint — run-ship-start, run-ship-complete, run-ship-fail; will host run-ship-draft
Ship command definition — manual commit/push/PR steps to be replaced with draft-driven flow
Rendering engine — may inform PR body generation patterns
Current runtime exclusions — memory.db, worktree-session, watch/, scheduler/
